summary:
I am JARVIS, a Python-based personal assistant project built for Prashant.
My main runtime is jarvis.py, with reusable helpers under jarvis_modules and tests under backend/tests.
I am made from voice input/output, AI chat and vision, Windows system control, camera, email, weather, news, notes, calendar, browser/automation, WhatsApp, Telegram, contacts, owner-face recognition, proactive prompts, and web/backend bridge pieces.
This self-index saw 197 files and recorded 192 safe entries while skipping secrets, sessions, caches, virtual environments, binaries, and large assets.
Key classes I found include: ActivityEvent, AudioAnalyzer, AudioMeter, BrowserMatchingTests, BrowsingAdapter, DiskAuditTests, ElevenLabsConfigTests, ElevenLabsTTSError, ExampleEnum, HudCard, JARVISGui, JarvisOrb, JarvisSecretary, JarvisWebHandler, LineChart, MetricBar, NotificationEvent, PersonalTelegramSecretary, PlaywrightAdapter, ProactiveEngine, RelayControl, ScrollColumn, SelfImprovementTests, SelfKnowledgeTests, ToastManager, VisionMode, WebBridgeCoreTests, WhatsAppBridge.
Important code files include: debug_sql.py, gemini-code-1778927298024.py, jarvis_gui.py, jarvis_gui_perf.py, jarvis_personal_telegram.py, jarvis_secretary.py, jarvis_telegram.py, jarvis_web.py, jarvis_whatsapp_bridge.py, playwright_check.py, playwright_test.py, proactive_engine.py, run_jarvis_bot.py, scratch_fetch.py, test_playwright.py, backend/__init__.py, backend/admin.py, backend/admin_endpoints.py, backend/agentic.py, backend/ai_tools.py, backend/browsing_adapter.py, backend/database.py, backend/main.py, backend/playwright_adapter.py, backend/security.py.

J.A.R.V.I.S GUI v3.0 Modern HUD-style desktop interface for the existing JARVIS backend. Features: - real-time embedded camera feed - capture live frame and use it as an AI reference image - attach files and folders as context for AI chat -

J.A.R.V.I.S GUI v3.0 Modern HUD-style desktop interface for the existing JARVIS backend. Features: - real-time embedded camera feed - capture live frame and use it as an AI reference image - attach files and folders as context for AI chat -

Personal Telegram secretary for J.A.R.V.I.S. This listens to the owner's Telegram account through Telegram's client API (Telethon), not the Bot API. It can therefore react to incoming personal DMs after the owner completes a one-time login.

Background Windows notification secretary for J.A.R.V.I.S. Import this module from jarvis.py and call: from jarvis_secretary import JarvisSecretary secretary = JarvisSecretary(jarvis_instance=self) secretary.start_background() The class del
Telegram secretary bot for J.A.R.V.I.S. This module is safe to import from jarvis.py. Call start_background() to run the bot in a daemon thread.

J.A.R.V.I.S web/PWA bridge. Run: python jarvis_web.py --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8765 For phone access on the same Wi-Fi: set JARVIS_WEB_TOKEN=choose-a-long-token python jarvis_web.py --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8765
Local WhatsApp Web bridge coordinator for J.A.R.V.I.S. The Node side owns WhatsApp Web. This Python side owns JARVIS voice prompts, cooldowns, and the conservative ask-before-send policy.
Optional automation/E2E dependencies for Jarvis Cowork validation.
Admin API endpoints - protected by X-Admin-Key header
AI Tools for J.A.R.V.I.S Cowork Mode. Provides the TOOLS_SCHEMA (OpenAI function-calling format) and execute_tool() so the AI brain can perform physical desktop actions via pyautogui.
Browsing adapter: supports 'requests' and 'playwright' providers. Playwright provider requires playwright package and browsers installed.

Minimal database module for J.A.R.V.I.S Cowork. Provides a SessionLocal factory used by execute_tool(). The computer_use tool doesn't need a real DB session, but the _create_completion call signature passes one in, so we provide a lightweig
Slack integration helpers (skeleton). Use /integrations/slack/install to build the install URL. This module is a helper; the FastAPI endpoints live in backend/main.py.
Backend regression tests for the JARVIS web bridge.
